name: Tortilla Stack
recipeIngredient: 0.5 lb Pork tenderloin, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, fry the tortillas in 1/4 inch of vegetable oil over medium-high heat for 10 to 15 seconds per side or until golden brown. Press down on any bubbles with a spatula and drain on paper towels.
2. Filling: Cook the pork in the oil over medium-high heat for 5 minutes or until no longer pink inside. Remove with a slotted spoon. Cook the onion and garlic over medium heat for 2 minutes or until softened. Stir in chili powder, cumin, oregano, and pepper, then cook for 30 seconds. Add the sweet peppers and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in the reserved pork, beans, and salsa.
3. In a separate bowl, stir together the sour cream, coriander, and remaining ingredients.
4. Place one tortilla on a baking sheet and spread with 3 tablespoons of the sour cream mixture. Top with one quarter of the pork mixture and 1/4 cup of cheese. Repeat layers, ending with another tortilla.
5. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 10 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining cheese and bake for an additional 3 minutes or until melted. Garnish with pepper strips. Let stand for 5 minutes before slicing. Serve with the remaining sour cream mixture.
Makes: 4-6 servings
